summary: VeriPatch v1.3.0 gap-closure is functionally complete locally but NOT production-ready until changes are committed, reviewed, and release consensus generated.

consensus.summary: VeriPatch v1.3.0 gap-closure is functionally complete locally but NOT production-ready until changes are committed, reviewed, and release consensus generated.
blockers: 20 uncommitted files — not release-ready until committed and reviewed
next_steps: Bump backend/pyproject.toml to 1.3.0 and update CHANGELOG.md
Commit gap-closure changes on staging with governance commit trailers
Open PR staging → main after CI green on GitHub
Manual smoke test: Update All with UAC on a Windows machine
Tag v1.3.0 on main after consensus JSON committed

agents.summary: Dispatched development-status workflow to all specialist agents.
Milestone v1.3.0-gap-closure is in progress; governance scaffold exists but changes are not committed.
Parallel fan-out completed: backend-architect + devops-automator ran concurrently.
Backend architecture is stable; one-click elevation flow added for Windows UAC relaunch.
CI/CD pipelines are green locally; governance job added to workflow.
Code quality is sound; uncommitted gap-closure work needs PR review before staging merge.
Documentation and governance artifacts are comprehensive; release notes pending for next version bump.

milestone.goal: Close VeriPatch requirements gaps: accessible UI, one-click elevation, and agent governance scaffolding
exit_criteria: All interactive GUI controls have descriptive tooltips and plain-language labels
Update All completes after one UAC approval when administrator access is required
governance/ tree exists with POLICY, milestones, knowledge-base, and delegation agent
CI runs scripts/check-governance-timestamps.py on every push and pull request
Windows installer build scripts finalized and MIT license verified in pyproject.toml
